The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $258,241,522Details
International Box Office $701,116,914
Worldwide Box Office $959,358,436
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $44,003,627
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $63,102,667
Total $107,106,294
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Having survived the beginning of their unexpected journey, the Company, consisting of Bilbo Baggins, the Wizard Gandalf, and thirteen Dwarves, led by Thorin Oakenshield, continues East, encountering along the way the skin-changer Beorn and a swarm of giant Spiders in the treacherous forest of Mirkwood. After escaping capture by the dangerous Wood-elves, the Dwarves journey to Lake-town, and finally to the Lonely Mountain itself, where they must face the greatest danger of all—a creature more terrifying than any other; one which will test not only the depth of their courage but the limits of their friendship and the wisdom of the journey itself—the Dragon Smaug.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$73,645,197 (28.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.51 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:26.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$250,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,903 opening theaters/3,928 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $379,488,456

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 13th, 2013 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
December 13th, 2013 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
October 7th, 2015 (Exclusive) by Warner Bros. / Fathom Events, released as The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug - Extended Edition
International Releases: December 12th, 2013 (Wide) (New Zealand)
December 12th, 2013 (Wide) (South Korea)
December 13th, 2013 (Wide) (Spain)
December 13th, 2013 (Wide), released as Hobbit: Smaug'un Çorak Toprakları (Turkey)
December 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: April 8th, 2014 by New Line Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for extended sequences of intense fantasy action violence, and frightening images.
PG-13 for extended sequences of intense fantasy action violence, and frightening images.
(Rating bulletin 2325, 6/4/2014)
Running Time: 201 minutes
Franchise: Peter Jackson's Lord of the Rings, The Hobbit
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Inbetwequel, Motion Capture Performance, Prequel
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Dragon, Dwarves, Elves, Escape, Invisibility, War
Source material: Lord of the Rings
Subgenre: Heroic Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Wingnut Films
Production Countries: New Zealand, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Having survived the beginning of their unexpected journey, the Company, consisting of Bilbo Baggins, the Wizard Gandalf, and thirteen Dwarves, led by Thorin Oakenshield, continues East, encountering along the way the skin-changer Beorn and a swarm of giant Spiders in the treacherous forest of Mirkwood. After escaping capture by the dangerous Wood-elves, the Dwarves journey to Lake-town, and finally to the Lonely Mountain itself, where they must face the greatest danger of all—a creature more terrifying than any other; one which will test not only the depth of their courage but the limits of their friendship and the wisdom of the journey itself—the Dragon Smaug.
